Phil taps into a rich new vein of clients - recently divorced women - but are they more trouble than they're worth? American comedy, starring Sofia Vergara, Eric Stonestreet and Ty Burrell

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Apu has the bright idea of turning the Kwik-E-Mart into a novel money-spinning tourist attraction, while Lisa is struck by the awful thought that her genetic make-up means she will follow in her family's footsteps and end up stupid

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is horrified at Marge's attempts to make him befriend Ralph Wiggum - until he learns his police chief dad has a master key to every shop in Springfield. In a bid to impress Nelson and his bully buddies, Bart and Ralph break into an abandoned prison, where they find an electric chair

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart decides he wants a little brother, and unofficially adopts a youngster named Charlie from the local orphanage - much to the dismay of Lisa. With the guest voices of Kim Cattrall (Sex and the City) and American Football stars Peyton and Eli Manning

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: During a trip to a water park with the family, Grampa bumps into a newspaper columnist who seemingly becomes fascinated with his life and writes a series of stories about him, but Homer becomes suspicious of the journalist's motives

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake makes an arrest without much proof, and so has to spend the next 48 hours collecting enough evidence to keep his suspect in custody. While his co-workers are forced to help him out over the weekend, Charles judges a pie contest between Rosa and Gina, and the Captain gives advice to Terry, who has a problem involving his brother-in-law. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake's dreams come true when he gets the chance to spend the day with his hero, veteran crime reporter Jimmy Brogan, but their encounter has unexpected consequences. Meanwhile, Terry and Charles prepare Rosa for a day in court, teaching her to be less terrifying on the witness stand. Guest starring Stacy Keach

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The adults take a trip to Las Vegas, where Jay wheels and deals to get them some better rooms - including their very own British butler, played by Stephen Merchant. Phil spends most of his time with his magician heroes - neglecting Claire in the process - and Mitch runs into an ex-boyfriend he dumped by e-mail, although it all seems fine when he invites Mitch and Cam to his bachelor party

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Plans for a relaxed evening at Jay's turn out to be anything but when Claire is mad with her dad over his lack of gratitude for her holding the fort at work and Mitchell is busy scheming after receiving a gift he doesn't like. Meanwhile, Phil and Gloria stop off at her old salon and get scissor-happy when a stylist fails to show up

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The Dunphys and Pritchetts head Down Under for a family holiday so Phil can fulfil his mother's wish that he should one day return to the country where he was conceived. Mitch and Cam meet up with an old friend who is now a big star, but neither of them can stand him, while Jay and Claire let work eat into their vacation time. Rhys Darby (Flight of the Conchords) guest stars

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The gang members get together to have their family photo taken, with Gloria on hand to organise everyone. and Phil tracks down the record he lost his virginity to, but soon regrets it when it makes him feel as if he's being unfaithful to Claire. She, meanwhile, accuses Cameron of being a snob when he refuses her daughter's hand-me-downs, and Jay enters Stella into a dog show

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Dad Who Knew Too Little
programme.desc: Homer realises he knows very little about Lisa and tries to fill the gaps in his knowledge by hiring a dodgy private detective to spy on her - only to end up on the wrong side of the law. With the guest voice of Elliott Gould

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer takes Ned Flanders' advice and starts praying regularly, hoping God will lend a sympathetic ear and make it possible for him to buy a better home. Marge warns that his materialistic attitude may incur divine retribution, but he turns a deaf ear - until a flood of biblical proportions hits Springfield

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart learns that he was once a child celebrity, but that Homer spent all the money he made, so he separates from his parents and moves into a loft apartment, where his neighbours are punk band Blink-182 and skateboarding hero Tony Hawk

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: I'm Spelling as Fast as I Can
programme.desc: Homer develops a ravenous appetite for Krustyburger's new spare rib sandwich, while Lisa is offered a scholarship at the college of her choice - but only if she agrees to deliberately lose a spelling contest

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer builds a robot for Bart to enter the Robot Rumble TV show, but finds the task harder than expected and resorts to trickery. Meanwhile, Lisa's pet cats keep dying

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer's new snowplough business proves an unexpected success but drives a wedge between him and Barney, who decides to operate a rival service. Featuring the voices of Linda Ronstadt and Adam West

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: One of the brides receives a surprise gift on her honeymoon in Panama. Two other newly-weds struggle to deal with a massive bombshell

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Dating guru Anna Williamson visits Coleen Nolan at her day job in the Loose Women studios, before sending her on a date with a joker. Lucinda Light goes axe throwing on her second date with bearded Damian. There are bound to be splinters

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Squid Game, Strictly Come Dancing, The Mating Game, Family Fortunes and Boris Johnson's appearance on The Andrew Marr Show, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A final romp through the Naked Attraction archive, remembering the times that the pickers got it spot on and chose their perfect date, leading to romance and happy endings. The success stories include Gemma and James, who found love on the show and whose relationship is still going strong

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Two more singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who will be revealed to them one body part at a time. Single mum Tracy from Bedfordshire has been out of the dating game since she split with her fiancee three years ago and hopes to find a man who matches her adventurous side, while London sales adviser Matthew, 24, is tired of dating apps and thinks online profiles mean girls never actually see the real him. Anna Richardson hosts

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Dating guru Anna Williamson visits Coleen Nolan at her day job in the Loose Women studios, before sending her on a date with a joker. Lucinda Light goes axe throwing on her second date with bearded Damian. There are bound to be splinters

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: One of the brides receives a surprise gift on her honeymoon in Panama. Two other newly-weds struggle to deal with a massive bombshell

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge goes on the offensive and plans a protest against a combat sport that has captivated Springfield's male population. When promoter Chett Englebrecht hears of her campaign, he challenges her to a fight - and agrees to shut down his entire operation if she beats him

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Dracula, the Wolf Man, the Mummy and Frankenstein's monster gatecrash a Halloween party at 742 Evergreen Terrace. Bart and Lisa plot revenge on their teachers in a Hitchcock-inspired short story, but end up turning on each another

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Adam takes Beverly to her high-school reunion, he tries to prevent a reality where she winds up with the wrong man

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri samples breakfasts from east to west. He has bagels in New Orleans, southern classics in New Mexico and then heads for pancakes in Pittsburg

programme.desc: Guy Fieri tries Italian dishes, including lasagne in Baltimore, pizza in San Diego and a family recipe for spaghetti in Pittsburg

programme.desc: Prue is joined by food campaigner and author Henry Dimbleby, who shows her how to make a one-pot veggie dish and lifts the lid on the school-dinner revolution he's been leading. Meanwhile, Prue's husband John is dispatched to catch dinner as he tries his hand netting trout at a nearby fish farm, while she makes an orange and polenta upside down cake

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer continues exploring culinary combinations by looking at the role of contrasting temperatures, demonstrating how they can improve dishes. He prepares recipes including apple pie and ice-cream, and fish with a lime and chilli vinegar, before meeting a chef in Scotland who cooks porridge with a twist

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer explores unusual ingredients and quirky twists on classic recipes, including a black-pudding sausage roll, a bread-and-butter dessert made from caramelised bananas, and a savoury apple crumble. In Derbyshire, Nigel also samples farmer Tom Clarke's signature boar burger

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree prepares a simple chicken and dressing sheet pan supper, lemon, basil and shrimp risotto in a skillet, the easiest ramen pho and a layered portable Greek salad in a jar

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree prepares a warming lunch, demonstrating how to make a chunky beef chili, and cheesy quesadillas

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates more stress-free recipes for tasty dishes that each contain only five ingredients, making quick Asian fishcakes with a brilliant tip for a sweet and spicy glaze. He also prepares a speedy lemony courgette linguine, sticky lamb chops as a single-pan dish, and cherry chocolate mousse for dessert

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates more stress-free recipes for tasty dishes that each contain only five ingredients, making a tender lamb shoulder for a sharing feast that is super-quick to prepare, as well as green spaghetti, a one-pan fish dish and apple crumble cookies.

programme.desc: Ina assembles a seasonal brunch. On the menu are morning glory muffins, a fresh fruit platter, shakshuka with feta, and a spicy bloody mary

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten shares inventive ways to cook with pork. On the menu are sausage strudels, butternut squash and herbed pork tenderloins

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The competition starts to heat up during chocolate week, as Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood set three tricky challenges to test the remaining bakers. There's a deceptively simple traybake in the signature, a technical packed full of chocolate and nuts, and a celebration showstopper that pushes the bakers' skills with white chocolate to the limit. Who will impress Prue and Paul enough to keep their place in the tent, and who will be heading home? Presented by Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates a simple version of a classic kedgeree, as well as breakfast drop scones, a chicken, avocado and bacon salad, and easy beefburgers. programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James crosses over the Irish Sea to Northern Ireland, where he is joined by famous local chef Paul Rankin. Their first port of call is the Giant's Causeway, where James cooks a fish stew for the crowds, followed by a visit to a family farm in Limavady, where Paul learns how their award-winning rapeseed oil is produced. Things get competitive as the chefs compete in a hovercraft race, and finally James takes to the seas to fish for mackerel

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Fellow chef Paul Rankin gives James a personal tour of Northern Ireland's capital city - his home town of Belfast - before the duo enjoy a spot of golf. James pitches up his mobile kitchen right on the green to whip up a tasty dish with bacon, scallops and apples with onion rings, and later cooks a Northern Irish classic - beef with champ, charred onions and parsley oil. Next, James is in Craigavon to visit Danni Barry, the first woman named the best chef in Ireland, who rustles up a dessert using local ingredients. Finally, the presenter visits Strangford Lough to fish for langoustines

programme.title: Faking It: Tears of a Crime

Dennis McArdle later admitted to killing Anne after admitting to a deal worth $20,000, and Danny Bowen took his own life while serving his prison sentence

programme.title: Swamp Murders

programme.title: Posse (1975)
programme.desc: A ruthless US marshal vows to capture a railroad bandit in a bid to further his political career. He ends up becoming the outlaw's hostage, and the men under his command turn to crime to raise the ransom money - which earns him the hatred of the voters. Western, directed by and starring Kirk Douglas. With Bruce Dern and Bo Hopkins

programme.title: The Mountain (1956)
programme.desc: Two hot-headed brothers set off to find the wreckage of an aircraft in the Alps - one intending to loot it, the other determined to stop him. But when they finally reach their goal, there's a surprise in store that forces the feuding siblings to deal with their differences once and for all. Drama, starring Spencer Tracy and Robert Wagner

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Captain Kirk is transported aboard an alien Enterprise, where assassination is a perfectly viable means of achieving promotion and mercy an unacceptable weakness. Starring William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy and DeForest Kelley

programme.desc: A robot duplicate of Oscar Goldman is used to steal the formula for a new energy source. Henry Jones guest stars

programme.desc: The rangers go undercover to investigate the death of a professional wrestler. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: Safe House (2012)
programme.desc: A CIA agent stationed in South Africa is sent to question a rogue operative, who has been arrested years after betraying the agency, only to be attacked by mercenaries hired to capture the fugitive. As they flee for their lives, the interrogator has to contend with not only the enemies hunting them, but also the mind games of his prisoner. Thriller, with Denzel Washington and Ryan Reynolds

programme.title: Drive Angry (2011)
programme.desc: A man breaks out of Hell when a satanic cult murders his daughter and kidnaps her baby. The infernal escapee pursues the leader of the fanatics, who plans to sacrifice the child to gain ultimate power - but he is being followed in turn by an agent of the Devil sent to take him back to his damnation. Fantasy thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Amber Heard and William Fichtner

programme.title: Villain (2020)
programme.desc: Former criminal Eddie Franks attempts to help his family by reconnecting with his daughter and clearing his brother's debt. Despite his efforts, he is drawn back into a criminal way of life, with devastating consequences. Director Philip Barantini's crime drama, starring Craig Fairbrass, Robert Glenister and George Russo

programme.title: War Pigs (2015)
programme.desc: A disgraced military officer is sent on a mission behind enemy lines during the Second World War. With the aid of a grizzled First World War veteran and a German Foreign Legion captain who has rebelled against the Nazi regime, he puts together a recon squad to find and capture a German secret weapon. Action adventure, starring Luke Goss, Dolph Lundgren and Mickey Rourke

programme.desc: Part two of two. Brady heads for the bright lights of San Francisco, to take part in a big card game. Western drama, starring Kenny Rogers and Reba McEntire

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Kirk comes across a paradise world where the natives live for ever and are guaranteed a never-ending supply of food - but discovers it may all be too good to be true. Final frontier adventures, starring William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy and James Doohan, with a guest appearance by David Soul

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: A woman is caught in a deadly conflict between Steve Austin and a posse of hunters

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: A police chase results in the death of the notorious 'Marina' rapist - but the officer refuses to believe that is the end of the story. Vintage American drama, starring William Shatner

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Alex is taken hostage by an officer framed for departmental corruption. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: State of Play (2009)
programme.desc: A hard-nosed journalist investigating the murder of a congressman's intern discovers a connection between the tragedy and the death of a petty crook on the same evening. As he works to get to the bottom of the story, the reporter uncovers a political conspiracy that places him in a precarious and potentially life-threatening position. Thriller, starring Russell Crowe, Ben Affleck, Helen Mirren and Rachel McAdams

programme.title: Nick of Time (1995)
programme.desc: A widowed businessman's beloved daughter is kidnapped by a terrorist and the only way he can get her back alive is to assassinate California's governor within 90 minutes. Thriller shot in real time by director John Badham, starring Johnny Depp, Christopher Walken, Marsha Mason and Charles S Dutton

programme.title: Red Heat (1988)
programme.desc: A Russian detective travels to America to oversee the extradition of a Georgian drug baron responsible for supplying cocaine in Moscow. He is teamed up with a hostile Chicago cop, but when their prisoner kills an officer and escapes, they put aside their mutual mistrust to bring him to justice. Action thriller, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, James Belushi, Peter Boyle, Laurence Fishburne and Ed O'Ross

programme.title: The Inglorious Bastards (1978)
programme.desc: Four American soldiers being transported to military prison through Nazi-occupied France escape when their convoy is attacked. Initially planning to escape to neutral Switzerland, they end up helping the French resistance steal a V2 rocket. Second World War thriller, starring Bo Svenson and Peter Hooten and Fred Williamson.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three boys are admitted for very different reasons, including cyclist Ollie, who arrives with head, neck and facial injuries having been involved in a traffic accident. Cameras also follow Dominic, another 16 year old. He was recovering at home after having his tonsils out when his throat started bleeding and he had to go to hospital

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Samuel, 24, is rushed to A&E after crashing his motorbike and being thrown into the road, while two-year-old Florence is brought in by her mum and dad, who suspect that she has swallowed a coin. Seventy-year-old Clive arrives after struggling to breathe at home, but his wife Linda has a difficult time keeping him in check, as he likes to tell a joke or two. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twenty-five-year-old Billy arrives at St George's by helicopter after being involved in a motorcycle accident in which a van ran over his chest. The team work quickly to get him into CT to see if he has any life-changing injuries, while his girlfriend, mum and stepdad desperately try to get to the hospital. Meanwhile, 58-year-old Stephen comes into A&E with his wife and son after showing signs of a stroke, and Milly brings her two-year-old son Freddie in after he jammed his thumb in a door

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. A woman has a blood pressure reading that's barely detectable. She is so ill, the paramedics are concerned that she may not survive a trip to hospital

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Andi Oliver and Matt Tebbutt journey through the islands of Barbados, Saint Lucia and Dominica to unearth the secrets of Caribbean food. In Barbados, they learn about rum production at Mount Gay, before finding out why hunting and eating lionfish could be good for the local ecosystem. In Saint Lucia the pair discover red bananas at a local market and head to Hotel Chocolat's Rabot Estate to find out why chocolate's flavour depends on where it is grown. They take a ferry to Dominica and discover a bread made by the Kalinago people with only the cassava root. Then, the duo meet a passion fruit farmer and discover why the fruit has such an evocative name

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attention to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Engaged couple Rosie Lewis and Dexter Green get the competition underway, welcoming their rivals to the Bae Abermaw Hotel in Barmouth, north Wales. Their guests are wowed by the views from their rooms, but Tracy finds herself in a hairy situation. At dinner, Rosie and Dexter's romantic revelations and confessions surprise their guests. Their breakfast sets the standard for the rest of the week, while Tracy sets herself up as the one to watch, with some observations about her room that she's keen to bring to everyone's attention

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Rigsby's Guest House in Doncaster, South Yorkshire, run by Tracy Ward, whose pricing system causes problems for a couple of guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Pub landlord John Adams and his friend Dennis Gowie welcome their rivals to The Somerset Arms in Semington, Wiltshire, where Rosie and Dexter have some cleanliness issues in their room

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nici Van Valen welcomes the contestants to the Cross Keys Hotel in Saffron Walden, Essex, for the final visit of the week, but a mark on Tracy's towel leaves a stain on her stay and a revelation at dinner leads to tension

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The Bae Abermaw Hotel is first and hosts Rosie and Dexter want to get to the bottom of Tracy's broken bath issue

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First aid manager Jacqui hosts the first dinner party in Perth, central Scotland, where she plans to impress her guests, optometric consultant Daniel, cabinet-maker Craig, administrator Lorea and procurement officer Jenna with her hearty cuisine, including a sticky toffee pudding

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring actor Daniel hosts the second dinner party in Perth, and hopes to impress his fellow contestants with a twist on the traditional Scottish menu - serving spaghetti hagginaise for his main and soft-drink flavoured ice-cream for dessert. But with outspoken guest Jenna admitting her dislike for the host, the attention is diverted away from the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: For the third party from Perth, Lorea is on a mission to showcase the culinary delights of her native home in northern Spain, and her fiesta commences with a pink cocktail and an exotic quartet flown over from the Basque country especially to impress. She reveals that she used to model in the nude, which perplexes her guests, and there are tensions at bay between Daniel and Jenna, which threaten to spoil the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Procurement officer Jenna hosts the fourth night of the competition in Perth, and tries to deliver the perfect evening. However, she struggles to hide her feelings toward aspiring actor Daniel after they have bickered all week, despite first aid manager Jacqui's attempts at reconciliation

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final day in Perth, and Kiwi Craig's turn to throw a dinner party before the winner is named. He hopes that his land and sea menu of prime lobster and wild boar will see him score highly, but hadn't counted on Jenna and Craig's tiara quarrel from the night before raging on into his evening. The host tries his best to calm the couple, but tensions mount to the point where cutlery is slammed down and one guest storms out

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition kicks off in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where management team Chris 'Clarkey' Clarke and Katie Light help run the 64-bedroom White Hart Hotel. When their guests arrive, Raouf and Chrissie from the New Promenade Hotel find out that they're staying in an outbuilding, while Robin and Liza Watson from Tall John's House love the modern gadgets in their room. In the afternoon, the guests meet some llamas and participate in a parade competition. Vicky from The Terrace makes an instant animal connection, which leaves quite an impression on her rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Raouf Elnagaar and his friend Chrissie Greenwood welcome their rivals to the budget option New Promenade Hotel in Blackpool. When they check out their rooms, Vicky finds a teetering toilet seat, Robin and Liza fall out of love with the budget feel and Clarkey and Katie are astonished to find filth on the taps. In the afternoon, the guests ride Blackpool's famous historic tram, but first they must get it ready for service with a wash and a scrub. Robin reveals his impressive family lineage when he spots the Blackpool Tower. At dinner, Raouf reveals his sensitive side, but his experience in hospitality fails to show

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week, Vicky Crawford welcomes her rivals to her three-bedroom terraced house in Oxford, aptly named The Terrace. But two pairs of guests discover an array of filthy finds, including thick dust, hairs and a pre-sucked sweet. In the afternoon the guests go punting, but Raouf's childish mood spoils their time on the river

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final visit is to Tall John's House, Robin and Liza Watson's grand Georgian mansion in the Welsh countryside, where the guests are wowed by the historic building and rooms. Raouf, Chrissie, Clarkey and Katie dig deep to find fault but can only find small problems. Following an afternoon in the billiard room, the guests gush over their friendship at dinner but, when it comes to the question of future meet-ups, Vicky drops a bombshell

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: White Hart Hotel management team Clarkey and Katie raise the issue of paying for their car park as the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit successful buyers from seasons past. In 2017 mother and daughter Linda and Laura bought a flat in Bristol with Phil's help that would suit Laura and her partner until they found their own home and Linda would move in with her husband when they retired. In 2018 Ranald and Marjorie were looking for a bigger home to hold family get-togethers with enough bedrooms for all the grandkids. Now Kirstie and Phil are back to see if the properties they bought were everything they hoped for

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the third heat, as more music enthusiasts from across the country are put to the test, from one hit wonders to chart-topping legends

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin goes behind the scenes at Chatsworth House, and visits Castell Coch, the Marquess of Bute's Victorian gothic fantasy, complete with painted monkeys and medieval extravagance. Kevin also takes a look at the Palace of Westminster, one of the nation's treasured icons, but now badly in need of repair

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-one-year-old Mark is brought in after coming off his bike and doctors are concerned he may have suffered life-changing damage to his spine, with wife Karen rushing to be by his side. Ten-year-old Aiden comes to paediatrics with his mum and her girlfriend Lorraine, after hurting his foot on the monkey bars at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Neil, 53, is rushed into hospital with severe chest and lung injuries after coming off his bike, and 40-year-old Michael is brought in with chest pain and a heart rate of up to 180bpm after collapsing on a golf course. Lastly, doctors treat 27-year-old George, who accidentally grazed his eye on a low hanging branch

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin goes behind the scenes at Chatsworth House, and visits Castell Coch, the Marquess of Bute's Victorian gothic fantasy, complete with painted monkeys and medieval extravagance. Kevin also takes a look at the Palace of Westminster, one of the nation's treasured icons, but now badly in need of repair

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-one-year-old Mark is brought in after coming off his bike and doctors are concerned he may have suffered life-changing damage to his spine, with wife Karen rushing to be by his side. Ten-year-old Aiden comes to paediatrics with his mum and her girlfriend Lorraine, after hurting his foot on the monkey bars at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three boys are admitted for very different reasons, including cyclist Ollie, who arrives with head, neck and facial injuries having been involved in a traffic accident. Cameras also follow Dominic, another 16 year old. He was recovering at home after having his tonsils out when his throat started bleeding and he had to go to hospital

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton and John Whaite go on an incredible journey to unearth the origins of the K-foods that have exploded in popularity in recent years. Starting in Seoul, they uncover the crispy secrets of Korean Fried Chicken and reveal the key ingredient in Gochujang sauce. Continuing south to Icheon, they investigate a rice-based alcoholic spirit that outsells all others in Korea. In Cheonan they discover the reason for the massive popularity of pork luncheon meat - aka spam - and find out how it's made. A short detour to the beach at Dacheon leads to a surprising and unforgettable seafood with a nickname fit for the playground. Finally, in Gwangju, they enter a world of kimchi, and learn the key to its global expansion

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Middlesex and Surrey, where projects include repurposing a 1950s hairdryer, updating a school desk and the restoration of a child's motorbike

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel the length of Britain turning unwanted junk into profit for their owners. In this episode they're on the south coast, where Henry gets to work an aircraft fuel tank while Simon gets obsessed with an iconic 1960s speedboat

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: General manager Garry Healey and housekeeper Zara Doherty welcome their rivals to the Cliffemount Hotel in Runswick Bay in North Yorkshire. Guests Lana and Alistair are wowed by the views, but Belgian sisters Daniele and Chantal don't think the décor reflects their surroundings. After an afternoon fossil hunting on the beach, Garry entertains his guests at dinner with a taste of his past, and gives them a sight to remember. In the morning, Claire and Adam think their breakfast lacks flavour, and the feedback leaves Garry and Zara wanting answers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Hospitality newcomers Alistair Arundel and Lana Keptanare welcome their rivals to the Quayside Hotel in Boston in Lincolnshire. Most of the guests are bowled over by the bling, but Claire feels like she's stuck in a neon nightmare. Feathers are ruffled during an afternoon of parrot-handling, with Garry and Zara feeling that Claire and Adam have shown their true colours. At dinner, the guests are impressed by Alistair and Lana's renovation story, but Alistair takes a dim view of Claire's LED criticism

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Lennon and Adam De Marco welcome their rivals to Hollington Park Glamping near Newbury, Berkshire. Sisters Daniele and Chantal are happy glampers but the other guests are shocked by the communal composting toilet. After an arty afternoon, Garry and Zara put aside their differences with their hosts. At dinner, Lana and Alistair don't hold back their feelings about camping, and at breakfast they reveal the full horror of their night under the stars. The feedback reveals that sharing isn't caring when it comes to the communal composting loo, and Claire and Adam have to face the fact that glamping isn't to everyone's taste

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sisters Daniele Spiers and Chantal Wirth welcome their rivals to Swan House in Kelvedon in Essex. The traditional decor impresses the guests, but there's cleanliness criticism all round, and Lana has wardrobe woes. During an afternoon of wine tasting, Claire and Adam reveal that their feedback hasn't left a bad taste in the mouth, and over dinner the group finds out more about Daniele's romantic past. In the morning, Garry and Zara wake up on the wrong side of bed after a disturbed night's sleep

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. East Walls Hotel is first up, and owners Celia and Damon need to clean up Dave's toilet brush troubles before broaching his beef with the breakfast banger

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Zumba teacher Amanda hosts the first dinner party in Weston-super-Mare, where she hopes to impress her guests Harry, drama teacher Nancy, businesswoman Tracy and estate manager Chris with a locally sourced Somerset menu. Although the evening starts off with some friendly banter, it soon turns sour for some of the contestants

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby fan and sports centre manager Harry hosts the second night in Weston-super-Mare, where he hopes to wow his guests with a traditional Hungarian menu, complete with beef in hunter's sauce and bird's milk with meringue dumplings

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, drama teacher and music lover Nancy throws an Austrian-themed party to reflect her love of the country and its music, with a menu featuring wiener schnitzel and venison stew. The starter proves to be a hit with the guests, but her main course fails to impress, and the atmosphere grows tense when Chris compares her dessert to the worst food experience of his life

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Farm-estate manager Chris hosts the fourth party in Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, and hopes his African-based menu can impress his guests. He serves savoury doughnuts, which are a new experience for his fellow diners. However, his main course of deep fried beef doesn't go down as well, leaving his chances of winning the competition resting on the evening's entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businesswoman Tracy hosts the final dinner party in Weston-super-Mare, and her dance-inspired evening includes lessons in cha-cha and tango as well as a black-tie dress code. Her guests tuck into a main course of steak cooked to each person's taste, which proves difficult to co-ordinate

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition starts at Martyn and Elaine Flint's lovingly decorated, adults-only B&B, the Hillcroft in Torquay. Guests Tony and Lisa find fault with their en suite, while Jorge demonstrates a keen eye for dust. The B&Bers share a lively game of bowls and at dinner, youngsters Sam and Henry's age and experience are subjected to scrutiny. Elaine faces a tough time in the kitchen and the feedback turns up the heat

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to the Bear and Swan in Chew Magna in Somerset, where Sam Wilkins and Henry Russell hope to impress with the five-star rooms above their country pub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Guesthouse East in Eastbourne, where Tony and Lisa Partridge hope to impress their guests with their family friendly B&B

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jorge Mesquita plays host at the Shirley B&B, Bournemouth, where he believes the guests will be impressed by his power showers and hopes a go-karting contest will help him win

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day arrives, and the hosts of The White Horse Inn, Barnfield B&B, The Black Swan Inn and Delamere Farm all discover just how much their guests valued their services

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with two sets of first time buyers who took on the market with their help. Elly and Paul from Bristol enlisted Phil's help in 2015 in their hunt for a period home with two double bedrooms, a bit of character and some outside space for future cats. Phil managed to find one with a cat flap already installed for £25,000 under-budget, now he visits them at a different property to find they have upsized in more ways than one. Kirstie met Steph and Nick in 2018 who were looking to buy their first home together after seven years of living in their own parents houses. With hopes of finding a futureproof home for them to settle in to, Kirstie found them a property in Dennyloanhead that was perfect for them to make their own, she returns to find out just how that went

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the fourth heat of Britain's toughest music quiz, as more contestants relive the hits and see if they really know the charts

programme.title: Great British Train Journeys from Above
programme.desc: The Ffestiniog Railway carves a path through the towering peaks of Eryri, one of Britain's most enchanting mountain ranges and changed the course of Welsh history. Travelling from the harbour town of Porthmadog to the historic slate mines at Blaenau Ffestiniog, Hugh Bonneville explores how the railways transformed a quiet corner of rural Wales into a global industrial powerhouse

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 54-year-old John, who was involved in a cycling accident in which he went over his handlebars and was unconscious for 10 minutes. Sydney, who's 87, has reluctantly come to A&E after he experienced severe chest pains, while Alan has fallen and badly fractured his elbow

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients whose visions of the world are rocked by life-changing events, including 44-year-old Sergio, who is flown in by air ambulance after a cycling accident. Another cyclist - 71-year-old Alan - arrives in resus after being in a collision with a van. Doctors suspect a head injury and multiple fractures. Alan's wife Jenny reveals how she met him and how a sudden accident involving her son burst their idyll and took their lives on an unexpected path

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman who has a history of heart complaints and is experiencing crushing pain in her chest. She thinks she may be having a heart attack. A bingo caller who has diabetes feels so unwell that his colleagues ring 999. A mother-of-nine is finding life a struggle and has collapsed on the kitchen floor with a suspected seizure. A former sergeant major and veteran of the Falklands war may be having a stroke. Plus, the paramedics encounter a condition that they cannot treat - a flat tyre

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients include 54-year-old John, who was involved in a cycling accident in which he went over his handlebars and was unconscious for 10 minutes. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final visit is to Tall John's House, Robin and Liza Watson's grand Georgian mansion in the Welsh countryside, where the guests are wowed by the historic building and rooms. Raouf, Chrissie, Clarkey and Katie dig deep to find fault but can only find small problems. Following an afternoon in the billiard room, the guests gush over their friendship at dinner but, when it comes to the question of future meet-ups, Vicky drops a bombshell

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: White Hart Hotel management team Clarkey and Katie raise the issue of paying for their car park as the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit successful buyers from seasons past. In 2017 mother and daughter Linda and Laura bought a flat in Bristol with Phil's help that would suit Laura and her partner until they found their own home and Linda would move in with her husband when they retired. In 2018 Ranald and Marjorie were looking for a bigger home to hold family get-togethers with enough bedrooms for all the grandkids. Now Kirstie and Phil are back to see if the properties they bought were everything they hoped for

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the third heat, as more music enthusiasts from across the country are put to the test, from one hit wonders to chart-topping legends

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin goes behind the scenes at Chatsworth House, and visits Castell Coch, the Marquess of Bute's Victorian gothic fantasy, complete with painted monkeys and medieval extravagance. Kevin also takes a look at the Palace of Westminster, one of the nation's treasured icons, but now badly in need of repair

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-one-year-old Mark is brought in after coming off his bike and doctors are concerned he may have suffered life-changing damage to his spine, with wife Karen rushing to be by his side. Ten-year-old Aiden comes to paediatrics with his mum and her girlfriend Lorraine, after hurting his foot on the monkey bars at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three boys are admitted for very different reasons, including cyclist Ollie, who arrives with head, neck and facial injuries having been involved in a traffic accident. Cameras also follow Dominic, another 16 year old. He was recovering at home after having his tonsils out when his throat started bleeding and he had to go to hospital

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a woman who has a history of heart complaints and is experiencing crushing pain in her chest. She thinks she may be having a heart attack. A bingo caller who has diabetes feels so unwell that his colleagues ring 999. A mother-of-nine is finding life a struggle and has collapsed on the kitchen floor with a suspected seizure. A former sergeant major and veteran of the Falklands war may be having a stroke. Plus, the paramedics encounter a condition that they cannot treat - a flat tyre

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-one-year-old Mark is brought in after coming off his bike and doctors are concerned he may have suffered life-changing damage to his spine, with wife Karen rushing to be by his side. Ten-year-old Aiden comes to paediatrics with his mum and her girlfriend Lorraine, after hurting his foot on the monkey bars at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three boys are admitted for very different reasons, including cyclist Ollie, who arrives with head, neck and facial injuries having been involved in a traffic accident. Cameras also follow Dominic, another 16 year old. He was recovering at home after having his tonsils out when his throat started bleeding and he had to go to hospital

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton and John Whaite go on an incredible journey to unearth the origins of the K-foods that have exploded in popularity in recent years. Starting in Seoul, they uncover the crispy secrets of Korean Fried Chicken and reveal the key ingredient in Gochujang sauce. Continuing south to Icheon, they investigate a rice-based alcoholic spirit that outsells all others in Korea. In Cheonan they discover the reason for the massive popularity of pork luncheon meat - aka spam - and find out how it's made. A short detour to the beach at Dacheon leads to a surprising and unforgettable seafood with a nickname fit for the playground. Finally, in Gwangju, they enter a world of kimchi, and learn the key to its global expansion

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole head to Middlesex and Surrey, where projects include repurposing a 1950s hairdryer, updating a school desk and the restoration of a child's motorbike

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel the length of Britain turning unwanted junk into profit for their owners. In this episode they're on the south coast, where Henry gets to work an aircraft fuel tank while Simon gets obsessed with an iconic 1960s speedboat

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: General manager Garry Healey and housekeeper Zara Doherty welcome their rivals to the Cliffemount Hotel in Runswick Bay in North Yorkshire. Guests Lana and Alistair are wowed by the views, but Belgian sisters Daniele and Chantal don't think the décor reflects their surroundings. After an afternoon fossil hunting on the beach, Garry entertains his guests at dinner with a taste of his past, and gives them a sight to remember. In the morning, Claire and Adam think their breakfast lacks flavour, and the feedback leaves Garry and Zara wanting answers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Hospitality newcomers Alistair Arundel and Lana Keptanare welcome their rivals to the Quayside Hotel in Boston in Lincolnshire. Most of the guests are bowled over by the bling, but Claire feels like she's stuck in a neon nightmare. Feathers are ruffled during an afternoon of parrot-handling, with Garry and Zara feeling that Claire and Adam have shown their true colours. At dinner, the guests are impressed by Alistair and Lana's renovation story, but Alistair takes a dim view of Claire's LED criticism

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Lennon and Adam De Marco welcome their rivals to Hollington Park Glamping near Newbury, Berkshire. Sisters Daniele and Chantal are happy glampers but the other guests are shocked by the communal composting toilet. After an arty afternoon, Garry and Zara put aside their differences with their hosts. At dinner, Lana and Alistair don't hold back their feelings about camping, and at breakfast they reveal the full horror of their night under the stars. The feedback reveals that sharing isn't caring when it comes to the communal composting loo, and Claire and Adam have to face the fact that glamping isn't to everyone's taste

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sisters Daniele Spiers and Chantal Wirth welcome their rivals to Swan House in Kelvedon in Essex. The traditional decor impresses the guests, but there's cleanliness criticism all round, and Lana has wardrobe woes. During an afternoon of wine tasting, Claire and Adam reveal that their feedback hasn't left a bad taste in the mouth, and over dinner the group finds out more about Daniele's romantic past. In the morning, Garry and Zara wake up on the wrong side of bed after a disturbed night's sleep

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. East Walls Hotel is first up, and owners Celia and Damon need to clean up Dave's toilet brush troubles before broaching his beef with the breakfast banger

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Zumba teacher Amanda hosts the first dinner party in Weston-super-Mare, where she hopes to impress her guests Harry, drama teacher Nancy, businesswoman Tracy and estate manager Chris with a locally sourced Somerset menu. Although the evening starts off with some friendly banter, it soon turns sour for some of the contestants

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Rugby fan and sports centre manager Harry hosts the second night in Weston-super-Mare, where he hopes to wow his guests with a traditional Hungarian menu, complete with beef in hunter's sauce and bird's milk with meringue dumplings

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, drama teacher and music lover Nancy throws an Austrian-themed party to reflect her love of the country and its music, with a menu featuring wiener schnitzel and venison stew. The starter proves to be a hit with the guests, but her main course fails to impress, and the atmosphere grows tense when Chris compares her dessert to the worst food experience of his life

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Farm-estate manager Chris hosts the fourth party in Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, and hopes his African-based menu can impress his guests. He serves savoury doughnuts, which are a new experience for his fellow diners. However, his main course of deep fried beef doesn't go down as well, leaving his chances of winning the competition resting on the evening's entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businesswoman Tracy hosts the final dinner party in Weston-super-Mare, and her dance-inspired evening includes lessons in cha-cha and tango as well as a black-tie dress code. Her guests tuck into a main course of steak cooked to each person's taste, which proves difficult to co-ordinate

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition starts at Martyn and Elaine Flint's lovingly decorated, adults-only B&B, the Hillcroft in Torquay. Guests Tony and Lisa find fault with their en suite, while Jorge demonstrates a keen eye for dust. The B&Bers share a lively game of bowls and at dinner, youngsters Sam and Henry's age and experience are subjected to scrutiny. Elaine faces a tough time in the kitchen and the feedback turns up the heat

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to the Bear and Swan in Chew Magna in Somerset, where Sam Wilkins and Henry Russell hope to impress with the five-star rooms above their country pub

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jorge Mesquita plays host at the Shirley B&B, Bournemouth, where he believes the guests will be impressed by his power showers and hopes a go-karting contest will help him win

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day arrives, and the hosts of The White Horse Inn, Barnfield B&B, The Black Swan Inn and Delamere Farm all discover just how much their guests valued their services

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with two sets of first time buyers who took on the market with their help. Elly and Paul from Bristol enlisted Phil's help in 2015 in their hunt for a period home with two double bedrooms, a bit of character and some outside space for future cats. Phil managed to find one with a cat flap already installed for £25,000 under-budget, now he visits them at a different property to find they have upsized in more ways than one. Kirstie met Steph and Nick in 2018 who were looking to buy their first home together after seven years of living in their own parents houses. With hopes of finding a futureproof home for them to settle in to, Kirstie found them a property in Dennyloanhead that was perfect for them to make their own, she returns to find out just how that went

programme.title: Great British Train Journeys from Above
programme.desc: The Ffestiniog Railway carves a path through the towering peaks of Eryri, one of Britain's most enchanting mountain ranges and changed the course of Welsh history. Travelling from the harbour town of Porthmadog to the historic slate mines at Blaenau Ffestiniog, Hugh Bonneville explores how the railways transformed a quiet corner of rural Wales into a global industrial powerhouse

programme.desc: With Britain standing alone, Churchill realises maintaining control of the vital oil supply line through the Suez Canal is critical to his nation?s hopes. After the fall of the strategically important port of Tobruk, Rommel only has the defensive line at El Alamein left to cross. programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver examines what impact the arrival of farming had on ancient social structures. He dives off the Isle of Wight where archaeologists have found an 8,000-year-old hunter settlement and learns what happened when hunter-gatherers met farmers for the first time. The historian reveals that when cultivation eventually came to Britain around 4,000 BC, it brought about the Neolithic era and a deep set of changes in people's way of life, from ground-breaking inventions like pottery, to new belief systems

programme.title: The Stuarts
programme.desc: After Charles I's disastrous attempt to impose political and religious uniformity throughout England, Scotland and Ireland, both the Stuart dynasty and its three kingdoms fell into an abyss. In this episode, Dr Clare Jackson reveals how the unprecedented religious violence of the Wars of the Three Kingdoms shaped British political culture and subsequent constitutional crises

programme.title: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programme.desc: With Britain standing alone, Churchill realises maintaining control of the vital oil supply line through the Suez Canal is critical to his nation?s hopes. programme.desc: As the US and Great Britain began to increase their military holdings while keeping their vanquished foes under-armed, it was at the expense of a humiliated Germany

programme.title: Plunderer: The Life and Times of a Nazi Art Thief
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jonathan Petropoulos investigates Nazi art dealer Bruno Lohse's life in post-war America. Dealing in looted art, he became an invisible hand, even as the original owners' families sought the return of works rightfully theirs. Featuring an archive interviews with Lohse himself, as well as curators, investigators and descendants of the victims of Nazi art theft

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver examines what impact the arrival of farming had on ancient social structures. He dives off the Isle of Wight where archaeologists have found an 8,000-year-old hunter settlement and learns what happened when hunter-gatherers met farmers for the first time. The historian reveals that when cultivation eventually came to Britain around 4,000 BC, it brought about the Neolithic era and a deep set of changes in people's way of life, from ground-breaking inventions like pottery, to new belief systems

programme.title: The Stuarts
programme.desc: After Charles I's disastrous attempt to impose political and religious uniformity throughout England, Scotland and Ireland, both the Stuart dynasty and its three kingdoms fell into an abyss. In this episode, Dr Clare Jackson reveals how the unprecedented religious violence of the Wars of the Three Kingdoms shaped British political culture and subsequent constitutional crises

programme.title: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programme.desc: With Britain standing alone, Churchill realises maintaining control of the vital oil supply line through the Suez Canal is critical to his nation?s hopes. After the fall of the strategically important port of Tobruk, Rommel only has the defensive line at El Alamein left to cross. Britain?s General Montgomery drives Rommel back from El Alamein, but that is only the beginning; America?s rookie troops make landfall in North Africa and join the fight

Giselle Esteban was later found guilty of murdering the 26-year-old after she became jealous of her relationship Le had with the father of her child

programme.title: 10 Steps to Murder
programme.desc: Pilot Iain Lawrence put his wife Sally on a pedestal. After he lost his job however, her position as a successful businesswoman was a constant reminder of the failure he perceived he had become. When their marriage fell apart, he refused to accept the loss of his wife - or of her wealth and status - and made plans to murder her

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: How, seven years into their marriage, something changed between a husband and wife - and a dirty secret at work led to a deadly confrontation

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: The fatal shooting of 23-year-old Michelle Lira, who steered straight into a deadly ambush by her vengeful ex-boyfriend, Selestino Torres, in June 2009

programme.title: American Detective with Lt Joe Kenda
programme.desc: On Chincoteague Island, a young mother is murdered steps from a vacant summer home. Det. Danny Plott learns she was lured to her death by a mysterious phone call

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the case of Rolandas Poskus, a man who was found dead in a park in Hull, and Amanda Playle, a woman who was stalked by her ex-boyfriend

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: When the body of Dawn Meade is found on the side of the road, detectives must determine if it is an accidental death or something more sinister

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: My Child the Killer
programme.sub-title: My Son is Coming to Kill Me
programme.desc: A father waits in terror, realising the hopes he had for his son are lost - his boy has killed his mother and might be coming to kill him too

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: The case of Susan Hamlin, who in 2004 walked into a police station to confess her part of a child abusing satanic cult with intentions to murder her husband. However, the truth proved even more bizarre and sinister as it became clear that her husband was the villain of the piece

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In Cleveland, Texas, a man guns down Conbino Crasto during a gas station robbery. CCTV sends police on a hunt for three masked suspects

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: After the brutal slaying of a homeless man, James Tony Wise, the Portland PD turns to the footage from surveillance cameras to find his killer

programme.title: Forensic Detectives
programme.desc: How cutting-edge technology is being used to solve murder cases more than a decade old, with a look at the kinds of information detectives search for when fresh clues are scarce

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: When a young man with a developmental disability vanishes, his legal guardian frantically searches for him. Police turn to CCTV to find him

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: Real-life cases reveal how video evidence has been used to solve a murder, as police reveal how CCTV footage has unlocked the answer to baffling cases

programme.title: Help! My House Is Haunted
programme.desc: It's a race against time for the team to help prevent an evil force from taking over a spiritually open woman in her Hampshire home

programme.title: Help! My House Is Haunted
programme.desc: Jon and Donna experience dark shadows, an oppressive atmosphere and pennies randomly appearing in their Yorkshire home. Is money the root of all evil?

programme.title: Help! My House Is Haunted

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: At a Cornish castle, Drew Pritchard digs out beach buckets and toy trucks. Later, his friend Stuart sells off his renowned collection of car memorabilia

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard and his long-time friend Tee John set out on a tour of France. In Normandy, Drew falls in love with a grand chateau and in a massive antique fair immerses himself in pop culture

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard and Tee John enjoy a tour of the Midlands. In Walsall, they have a money-spinning idea, in Birmingham Drew finds treasure and they also explore a grand country house

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Quirky items, including a secret society's ballot box, an uncle's gold tooth, and expensive kitchenware, create a buzz in the auction room

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth helps an RAF hero sell a rare SAS diary to raise cash for veterans. Plus, a man parts with 250 cars, hoping to fund the cruise of a lifetime

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Goldsmith Richard faces his toughest restoration yet, a beloved engagement ring. Meanwhile, metal master Domino revives a weathervane for Father Lesley

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott embrace their inner pirate as they plunder the shops of the Cornish peninsula. Raj makes some big finds in one of the tiniest shops of the trip and pins his hopes on a risky item that may or may not be highly collectable. Irita plumps for a very cheap purchase and finds a painting that reminds her of her childhood and also contains a hidden secret. Along the way there's still time for some sightseeing, kite-flying and, of course, pirate impressions. The pair then head to the final auction to see who will emerge victorious

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Charles Hanson head to the North of England, on the opening salvo of an epic expedition together. Their transportation is the oldest car ever to grace the trip, a 1932 Riley Monaco. Charles unearths a 19th-century Japanese find, and some items from closer to home, but further back in time. Natasha relies on some obscure primary-school knowledge to decode one of her finds

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow three stories of siblings brought together after decades apart. Last year the programme introduced John Nicklin to four siblings on his birth mother's side, and in the meantime he has uncovered secrets on his father's side of his family, leading to a whole new reunion. They also catch up brother and sister Tracey Collins and Christian Hilton, who reunited in 2014, as well as 81-year-old Pauline Wood as she prepares for her wedding day, with long-lost brother Colin set to give her away

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit more of the programme's most memorable searches, catching up with two people whose lives have been transformed. Kathleen Fraser Jackson was reunited with her birth mother in Canada, and the programme follows her to Jamaica to meet her youngest sister Peta Gay for the first time. The second story has never been shown before and features twins who were separated as babies

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.sub-title: Ringing Bells and a Rare Watch
programme.desc: Angus helps a grieving daughter clear her late mum's huge collection of bells. Another job sees a room emptied for the first time in 50 years

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Anita Manning and Raj Bisram head to Suffolk, Kent and Surrey to search for treasures, before returning to Essex for their third auction

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow Kathleen Fraser Jackson's search for her mother, with the 62-year-old having been trying to track down the woman who gave her up for adoption for more than 40 years. In a last-ditch attempt to help Kathleen find her mum, the Long Lost Family team samples Kathleen's DNA and adds it to an online database, which triggers an epic journey spanning continents and cultures, and unlocking more secrets - and family members - than she could ever have dreamed of

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: BBC world affairs editor John Simpson always thought his great-grandfather was Samuel F Cody, the Wild West entertainer who also achieved fame as the first person in Britain to conduct a powered flight. It was only later that he discovered his great-grandmother had run off with Cody, taking her children with her. John follows the trail of his adventurous relative and her life with the cowboy-turned-aviator. He also investigates what happened to the husband she left behind - John's real great-grandfather - a search that leads to a shocking discovery

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and his team of experts meet Liz from Dorset who brings in a crumbling cast-iron gate built by her brother as a gift for her and her late husband. Horologist Steve Fletcher and leather expert Suzie Fletcher are brought a century-old watch by Nancy who's father was awarded the watch for his bravery on his return from the First World War. Upholsterer Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on a rocking chair that has been at the heart of Anna's family home for decades and is in desperate need of some TLC. Last into the shop is Ruth and her Auntie Roz who bring in a penny arcade game from the 1920s

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Charles Hanson explore the East Riding of Yorkshire, where Charles finds a few goodies on board a vintage double decker bus and learns all about the link between Dracula and the seaside town of Whitby. Meanwhile, keen ornithologist Natasha visits the coastal haven of Bempton Cliffs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Charles Hanson travel from East to West Yorkshire, where away from the antiques search Charles plays a game of cricket and learns about the humble Yorkshire pudding. Meanwhile, Natasha pays a visit to Leeds, where it is believed the first ever moving pictures were created, half a decade by Edison and the Lumiere brothers

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow the story of reunited siblings Geoffrey Tonks and Barbara Jacobs as they investigate a family mystery and try to trace a missing sister on Geoffrey's father's side. They also catch up with Robert Lindsay, who explores the difficulties of building a father/son relationship so late in life, and brother and sister Nicholas Rhoades and Cayley Cox talk about how they have already experienced an enormous amount together in the year that has passed since they met for the first time

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow three stories of sons coming to terms with the past, including Marc Wolfe's struggle to form a bond with his birth mother Esther, who lives in New Zealand. However, his search for identity didn't end there as researchers were able to reunite him with his birth father David, and the pair have grown close. One significant hurdle remained in the form of introducing David to his adoptive father Terry, and cameras were there as Marc introduces his two dads to each other

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Master builder Harrison Gardner helps newly married couple Aoife NicChraith and Louis Brennan, who have plans for an ambitious modern extension to their one-bedroom cottage in Tuam, County Galway. They are essentially attaching a new three-bedroom house to a 200-year-old house to cater for their growing family

programme.title: The Irish for Sex
programme.desc: Comedian Bláithín de Burca searches nationwide to find words for women's bodies and sexuality in Gaeilge. She explores the Irish language of the past, present and future to find womens' words in the hopes of unlocking a uniquely Irish experience of womanhood. Featuring interviews with guests, including Manchán Magan, Dr Sharon Arbuthnot of Cambridge University and The Wild Geeze

programme.title: The Dry
programme.desc: As Bernie and Daryl spend more time together, Shiv starts to feel left out. She is determined to prove to Daryl that she can still be fun too. Meanwhile, Caroline continues to try to take her life back into her own hands, making some questionable decisions along the way. Tom makes a bat for his new future with Ant's help, while Bernie revisits the past with a new lens, with the help of Daryl

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Retired heptathlon star Jessica Ennis-Hill sets out to discover the truth behind a family mystery concerning her great-great-grandmother. When his father, Jess's great-grandfather was a child his mother Emily Maud, known as Maud, walked out. The family never talked about Maud, and Jess's grandfather has no idea what became of her. Jess wants to investigate what could have happened to make her two-time great-grandmother abandon her children and effectively be written out of the family's history. She also examines her Jamaican four-time great-grandfather's journey from enslavement to land ownership

programme.title: The Jetty
programme.desc: Still reeling from her shocking discovery, Ember is desperate to find out what Riz knows about the Amy Knightly case. They begin to share information and Ember tells Riz her suspicions about the identity of Miranda's abuser. Ember is horrified when she's told that the lake is to be searched for Amy's remains. When Riz stops returning Ember's calls, Ember suspects that it's because she has uncovered the terrible truth about what happened to Amy. Crime drama, starring Jenna Coleman and Weruche Opia

programme.title: The Irish for Sex
programme.desc: Comedian Bláithín de Burca searches nationwide to find words for women's bodies and sexuality in Gaeilge. She explores the Irish language of the past, present and future to find womens' words in the hopes of unlocking a uniquely Irish experience of womanhood. Featuring interviews with guests, including Manchán Magan, Dr Sharon Arbuthnot of Cambridge University and The Wild Geeze

programme.title: The Dry
programme.desc: As Bernie and Daryl spend more time together, Shiv starts to feel left out. She is determined to prove to Daryl that she can still be fun too. Meanwhile, Caroline continues to try to take her life back into her own hands, making some questionable decisions along the way. Tom makes a bat for his new future with Ant's help, while Bernie revisits the past with a new lens, with the help of Daryl

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Retired heptathlon star Jessica Ennis-Hill sets out to discover the truth behind a family mystery concerning her great-great-grandmother. When his father, Jess's great-grandfather was a child his mother Emily Maud, known as Maud, walked out. The family never talked about Maud, and Jess's grandfather has no idea what became of her. Jess wants to investigate what could have happened to make her two-time great-grandmother abandon her children and effectively be written out of the family's history. She also examines her Jamaican four-time great-grandfather's journey from enslavement to land ownership

programme.title: Ballywalter (2022)
programme.desc: Following the painful breakdown of his marriage, lost soul Shane nervously enrols in a stand-up comedy course. During repeated journeys to classes, he strikes up a friendship with unlicensed taxi driver Eileen, who is making ends meet behind the wheel of her ex-boyfriend's minicab. They inspire one another to clamber out of their respective ruts and look enthusiastically to the future. Drama starring Seana Kerslake and Patrick Kielty

programme.title: Here We Go
programme.desc: With the new year fast approaching, Rachel is keen to throw a massive party, although Paul would much rather the family welcomed in 2026 in a quiet holiday cottage - preferably one with a sauna so he can sweat out the massive boil on his cheek. programme.desc: A hapless groom-to-be appears to have finally won the approval of his stern prospective in-laws, but faces a new challenge when he takes them on a vacation to Florida to meet his free-spirited sex therapist mother and eccentric house-husband father. programme.title: Tony Connelly: A Hidden History
programme.desc: Armed with a few fading photos and a handful of family stories, the journalist sets out to uncover the astonishing life of his grandfather. Michael Connelly was a constable in the highly controversial Royal Irish Constabulary during the War of Independence, and later a sergeant in Northern Ireland's newly formed - and mainly Protestant - police force

programme.title: From that Small Island
programme.sub-title: New Worlds: The First Irish Diaspora
programme.desc: The documentary piecing together the jigsaw of Ireland's earliest inhabitants focuses on the century from 1600 to 1700, when continental Europe was the main destination for tens of thousands of Irish migrants. This was the first Irish diaspora

programme.title: Impact: Anatomy of a Car Crash
programme.desc: In December 2016, joy and tragedy collided on Military Road outside Dungarvan, County Waterford. One car carried a Cork family on their way to see Santa at Winterval and the other carried three young men, whose lives would be extinguished in an instant. This documentary revisits one of the most devastating road collisions in Irish history, laying bare the enduring physical, emotional and psychological scars left in its wake

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Documentary following stately home owners who hire out their estates to earn extra income, and the clients who pay to use their properties for functions including stag parties, weddings and hunting trips. The programme explores how the reality of life in Britain's grand houses is often less glamorous than it appears, as proprietors struggle to maintain crumbling buildings and pay utility bills, and examines the modern-day divide between domestic staff and their employers. Featuring life at Chillingham Castle, Maunsel House, Carlton Towers and Muncaster Castle

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capital's love hotels. programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the city's biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room
