Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale and Maurice Denham

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard take over a country cottage for a couple of days, with unexpected results. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: A visit to a local country mansion reveals Boycie's admiration for the long-established tradition of respect for the gentry. Elgin moves into Bryan's caravan after being thrown out by his wife, leading to relentless gossiping by Mrs Cakeworthy. When the mobile home burns down Marlene offers the two men a place to stay, much to her husband's chagrin. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin announces Gladys and Spike's engagement to the local papers - much to their annoyance - but all is not as it seems. Comedy, starring Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring orders Wilson to bring the platoon back from a grudge match against the ARP wardens' darts team and get the men back on parade. The captain then takes a call warning of a suspected terrorist in Walmington-on-Sea and decides to deal with the situation himself. Comedy first broadcast in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: After complaining about their long wait for lunch in the canteen, the staff are told to take over the kitchens themselves - but when they arrive for their shift, they find the area unhygienic. Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden, John Inman and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville and Gastric plan to persuade Madge and Mavis to stay by treating them to a romantic evening in a fancy restaurant. However, it involves spending a little more money than the shopkeeper is comfortable with. Eric and Cyril try to make their wives jealous, while Leroy worries about the prospect of introducing Beth to his father

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Arkwright loses his trousers on the way to the wedding of Nurse Gladys Emmanuel's niece, and ends up having to borrow a pair of her trousers to wear at the event

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring orders Wilson to bring the platoon back from a grudge match against the ARP wardens' darts team and get the men back on parade. The captain then takes a call warning of a suspected terrorist in Walmington-on-Sea and decides to deal with the situation himself. Comedy first broadcast in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy persuades Clegg and Compo that starting a dog-walking business will be no work at all since they are already going on daily countryside rambles. However, the experience proves to be more of a workout than the trio envisage. Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy, Clegg and Compo hit on a solution to their transport problems - building their own bikes. Meanwhile, Sid and Ivy's cafe has been redecorated and is set for its grand re-opening. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth and Richard take over a country cottage for a couple of days, with unexpected results. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring and his platoon set up an observation post in the local lighthouse, but manage to annoy ARP warden Hodges by inadvertently flooding the whole town with light during an air raid. Comedy first shown in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Open All Hours: Inside Out
programme.desc: Diane Morgan narrates this special documentary about the much-loved comedy, featuring contributions from Sir David Jason, Johnny Vegas, Tim Healy, Ronnie Barker's daughter Charlotte, and more

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Gastric spends the night at Madge's house, but has he found the path to her best bedroom? Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain, not knowing exactly what is in the very large crate. Eric and Cyril have a fall out over a lost compass, while Mr Newbold falls in and gets a soaking on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi. The canny grocer has his work cut out getting friends to make up and getting a wet customer home without being spotted by 'Black Widow' Mrs Featherstone. Comedy, starring David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy, Johnny Vegas and Sue Holderness

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy commits a minor indiscretion while drunk, but claims to have no memory of the event. Determined to get to the bottom of the affair, she and Lee embark on an ambitious quest to discover the truth about whether it is possible to retain their memories of a heavy night of drinking - making use of liberal amounts of home-made potato hooch. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A 20-year-old woman turns up on Lee's doorstep asking to borrow a cup of sugar. However, she has an ulterior motive - young Debbie believes Lee may be her father. After a period of denial, he remembers a one-night stand from a celebratory party thrown by Tim at the end of his accountancy exams in the 1980s. Sitcom, starring Lee Mack, Tim Vine and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee imagines having the perfect life with Lucy, but the reality may not be good enough to match up to his dreams. Comedy, guest starring Bobby Ball, with Lee Mack, Tim Vine, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix

programme.title: Don't Wait Up
programme.desc: Tom decides he cannot cope living with his father after they argue over domestic issues. Nigel Havers and Tony Britton star

programme.title: Black Ops
programme.desc: Police community support officers Dom and Kay are tasked to infiltrate a predominantly black gang of villains. Working undercover, the pair are unwittingly thrust into the murky world of deep cover infiltration as they pose as disgruntled former officers within a powerful criminal enterprise, with the motiviations of the DI who sent them in becoming increasingly unclear. Comedy, starring Gbemisola Ikumelo and Hammed Animashaun

programme.title: The Outlaws
programme.desc: Frank looks back on his glory days in the 1980s and reflects on his choices. As the facts about the robbery become clearer, Haines and Selforth find an eager new partner in Diane. Shanthi and Jerzy turn against Rani, inadvertently offering an opportunity to the local drug boss. Greg must go above and beyond the call of duty to help his new friends out - whether he wants to or not

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot offers his body and Gary fixes up a burglar alarm in forlorn efforts to impress Deborah

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In 2007, when Paris Bennett was 13 years old, stabbed his four-year-old sister to death, a crime for which he has been behind bars ever since. Now 25 and in his first interview on British television, he tells Piers Morgan how this was no impulsive fit of rage but rather a cold, deliberate act planned as an act of revenge against his mother Charity - who is watching the interview unfold in a nearby room. She has stood by him even though she fears that when he gets out, he could still harbour violent intentions towards her - or even worse, towards his younger brother

programme.title: Lies, Deceit and Betrayal
programme.desc: After Louise meets her partner Andy Camfield, a series of financial mishaps begin to see her regularly giving him money

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: When a Florida couple goes missing, the discovery of a grisly bait freezer leads investigators to a surprise informant who claims the truth is hidden at sea

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners meet Sue, who helped build a lesbian-safe section of Kansas City in the 80s and hasn't been able to tidy up since the love of her life died

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Dudley, Ash and Jamie blue light to a frightened young woman with severe chest pains. Rachel and Mick are called to assist the police with a prisoner in Stoke

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Homes
programme.desc: The Scott brothers renovate a house for couple Allie and Ben that used to belong to Allie's grandparents

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, helps a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a small house. Sandra's daughter Crystal Chambers passed away in 2015, so she and Crystal's 19-year-old brother Ziggy are doing their best to raise youngsters Deago and Ameira amid cramped conditions

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nick Knowles and the team undertake their biggest and longest build yet, constructing two buildings for the Grenfell community over several months to create spaces worth around £2million. With the aid of large construction companies, generous suppliers, volunteers and the Duke of Cambridge - who visits the site - the team starts by building a new home for the Dale Youth Boxing Club. The club started the careers of James DeGale, who won an Olympic gold medal in Beijing 10 years ago, and George Groves, current WBA Super Middleweight champion, as well as producing many national champions

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nick Knowles and the team attempt to build a two storey flexible use centre, which can be used by the local community however it decides. The team are joined by the Duke of Cambridge, who meets local residents and talks to firefighters who were amongst the first responders on the night of the tragic fire at Grenfell Tower

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats Koa, a six-month-old Bouvier des Flandres who is suffering from a badly deformed back leg, causing huge pain. Patricia brings her beloved 11-year-old Rhodesian ridgeback, Kwanda, to Fitzpatrick's Guildford hospital with an eye infection that she fears may be the sign of something more sinister. Dr Laurent Findji confirms it is bad news - a malignant tumour

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats an 11-month-old dog who has a severely dislocated hip which Noel suspects is the result of trauma experienced at the tender age of only two months. Elsewhere, overweight Labradoodle Fletcher is brought into the veterinary practice all the way from Scotland after previous surgery to fix a bad ankle fracture has failed, and a tiny hedgehog needs treatment on his broken hind legs

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, helps a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a small house. Sandra's daughter Crystal Chambers passed away in 2015, so she and Crystal's 19-year-old brother Ziggy are doing their best to raise youngsters Deago and Ameira amid cramped conditions

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets a litter of puppies who were found abandoned in a crisp box, and helps the staff fight to keep them alive

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: Morgan starts working at a remote Scottish eco-resort, but Annika's trip to visit her is interrupted when a local builder employed by the resort turns up dead in a nearby stream

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over
programme.desc: The investigative reporter aims to find out what life is like in modern Britain by spending 72 hours in the company of a wide range of extraordinary characters and families. She begins in Shepherd's Bush, where she visits married couple Thomas and Cathy and their seven-year-old son Stanley. Cathy and Thomas have an open relationship and live a polyamorous life - they also share their home with Australian Nicole, who is part of their relationship

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and TV personality Piers Morgan continues his travels across the USA, this time meeting two very different female murderers, both of whom killed someone they loved. First, he heads to Florida's largest female prison to visit Amanda Hutto, who is serving a life sentence for murdering her daughter. She was accused of deliberately 'dunking' her seven-year-old child as a punishment for being naughty, with her young son testifying against her at the murder trial. Next, he journeys to Texas, where he encounters Rhonda Glover, a former beauty queen and rodeo star who gunned down her lover in cold blood after their relationship spiralled into drug-fuelled obsession and paranoia

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and broadcaster Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of America's most notorious female killers in a quest to discover what drives women to kill. He travels to Gatesville, Texas, to visit Erin Caffey, who masterminded the murder of her entire family at the age of 16, and will remain behind bars until she is almost 60. Piers finds out what made a typical teenager conspire with her boyfriend to kill her mum, dad, and two younger brothers, before meeting the only survivor of the tragedy, Erin's father Terry

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: When the body of a young woman is found in the coastal waters off Cape May, the chains binding her ankles could be the missing link to solving her murder

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Godfrey face his grief by helping him clean a basement full of his deceased parents' things that he doesn't want to get rid of

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Anna and Stuart rush to the home of a former soldier who has fallen down some stairs and has a potential spinal injury. Ben and Laura attend a young boy with suspected appendicitis

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Homes
programme.desc: Jen and Alex's 1940s LA home is full of character and still has some of the original features, but its closed-off layout is no longer functioning for the growing family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team helps project manager Ben in Avening in the Cotswolds, whose life was changed in a split second by a freak accident while holidaying in Cyprus. Ben slipped and fell head first into the shallow end of a pool, breaking his neck in three places, and for him to move back home from Salisbury Spinal Unit, it needs to be adapted with a front access and a two-storey extension with through-floor lift, along with all the bespoke care provision that he will need, including a home-office space

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In March 2015, football fan Simon Dobbin was at an away game when he was set upon by a gang of men in an unprovoked attack that left him brain damaged, paralysed and unable to walk and talk. After many months in hospital he is finally back at home in Mildenhall, Suffolk, where he receives round-the-clock care from wife Nicole. But the family home is unfit for Simon's care and rehabilitation - so that's where Nick Knowles, designer Laurence Llewelyn- Bowen and the DIY SOS team come in, strengthened by a number of local tradesmen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Hessle, near Hull in East Yorkshire, to transform the home of Darren Hudson, who was left paralysed from the waist down due to a spinal cord injury. After three months of hospital rehabilitation, Darren returned home to his family, but tragedy struck again eight days later when his wife Sarah died suddenly. Since that moment, Darren and his three sons have been living in emotional trauma, with the wheelchair-using dad now the main carer for the family

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel suspects that an 18-month-old Australian Shepherd dog may be suffering from nerve compression, and an 11-month-old pug shows signs of neurological damage. It transpires that she has bone malformation that is squashing her spinal cord. Elsewhere, there is an emergency admission when a Jack Russell puppy called Bella is rushed in with a crushed pelvis after a freak accident

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog called Dave has a spinal deformity, and Noel discovers that not only does he have a genetic malformation of his sacral and lumbar vertebrae, but also excess bone in the pathways where the nerves travel to his legs. Meanwhile, a six-year-old terrier has lost a paw after being attacked by another dog, and the only options appear to be replacing the paw with a bionic implant or amputating the entire limb

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Fortune smiles on Mike and Alison when a TV company comes calling, looking for an old house in which to film a period drama. But to get the money in the bank, Alison must ensure the shoot goes without a hitch despite the horde of curious ghosts, the house's crumbling floorboards and her crippling crush on the show's lead actor. Meanwhile, Thomas and Lady Button learn the meaning of a free pass, and Julian tries his luck at betting on horse racing. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Learning that their posh neighbours the Beg-Chetwyndes have an ancient claim over their land, Mike and Alison throw an upmarket dinner party to help resolve a thorny issue. But with the ghosts her only guide to high-class decorum, the evening starts badly and only gets worse - thanks to Mary's woeful catering, Mike's clueless deal-making and Robin's raucous celebration of the lunar eclipse. Elsewhere, the spooks become obsessed with a TV sitcom. Comedy, guest starring Geoff McGivern and Sophie Thompson

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of America's most notorious female murderers, in a quest to discover what drives a woman to kill. He travels to Lowell Correctional Institute in Florida to talk to Rebecca Fenton, convicted for the murder of her husband Larry, who was shot dead in their home. He also looks into the details of the case, learning how for several years it seemed she was going to get away with the crime, until the involvement of the FBI helped bring her to justice

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: In the wake of a violent storm, a Rhode Island harbourmaster finds a badly decomposed body aboard a sailboat

programme.sub-title: The Death Cleaners vs the Sister Council
programme.desc: A couple who have raised eight children are ready to embrace the death cleaning process, but the children are against the move

programme.desc: V and Ollie blue light to a rugby pitch to help a man who had a seizure. Plus, Stef and Lee treat an excitable 6-year-old Spider-Man fighting an asthma attack

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats Koa, a six-month-old Bouvier des Flandres who is suffering from a badly deformed back leg, causing huge pain. Patricia brings her beloved 11-year-old Rhodesian ridgeback, Kwanda, to Fitzpatrick's Guildford hospital with an eye infection that she fears may be the sign of something more sinister. Dr Laurent Findji confirms it is bad news - a malignant tumour

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats an 11-month-old dog who has a severely dislocated hip which Noel suspects is the result of trauma experienced at the tender age of only two months. Elsewhere, overweight Labradoodle Fletcher is brought into the veterinary practice all the way from Scotland after previous surgery to fix a bad ankle fracture has failed, and a tiny hedgehog needs treatment on his broken hind legs

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and team, together with West Bromwich volunteers, helps a grieving grandmother and her family who are making do in a small house. Sandra's daughter Crystal Chambers passed away in 2015, so she and Crystal's 19-year-old brother Ziggy are doing their best to raise youngsters Deago and Ameira amid cramped conditions

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nick Knowles and the team undertake their biggest and longest build yet, constructing two buildings for the Grenfell community over several months to create spaces worth around £2million. With the aid of large construction companies, generous suppliers, volunteers and the Duke of Cambridge - who visits the site - the team starts by building a new home for the Dale Youth Boxing Club. The club started the careers of James DeGale, who won an Olympic gold medal in Beijing 10 years ago, and George Groves, current WBA Super Middleweight champion, as well as producing many national champions

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets a litter of puppies who were found abandoned in a crisp box, and helps the staff fight to keep them alive

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: Morgan starts working at a remote Scottish eco-resort, but Annika's trip to visit her is interrupted when a local builder employed by the resort turns up dead in a nearby stream

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over
programme.desc: The investigative reporter aims to find out what life is like in modern Britain by spending 72 hours in the company of a wide range of extraordinary characters and families. She begins in Shepherd's Bush, where she visits married couple Thomas and Cathy and their seven-year-old son Stanley. Cathy and Thomas have an open relationship and live a polyamorous life - they also share their home with Australian Nicole, who is part of their relationship

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In 2007, when Paris Bennett was 13 years old, stabbed his four-year-old sister to death, a crime for which he has been behind bars ever since. Now 25 and in his first interview on British television, he tells Piers Morgan how this was no impulsive fit of rage but rather a cold, deliberate act planned as an act of revenge against his mother Charity - who is watching the interview unfold in a nearby room. She has stood by him even though she fears that when he gets out, he could still harbour violent intentions towards her - or even worse, towards his younger brother

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Piers Morgan travels across the USA to meet some of America's most notorious female murderers, in a quest to discover what drives a woman to kill. He travels to Lowell Correctional Institute in Florida to talk to Rebecca Fenton, convicted for the murder of her husband Larry, who was shot dead in their home. He also looks into the details of the case, learning how for several years it seemed she was going to get away with the crime, until the involvement of the FBI helped bring her to justice

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: When the body of a young woman is found in the coastal waters off Cape May, the chains binding her ankles could be the missing link to solving her murder

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Godfrey face his grief by helping him clean a basement full of his deceased parents' things that he doesn't want to get rid of

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Anna and Stuart rush to the home of a former soldier who has fallen down some stairs and has a potential spinal injury. Ben and Laura attend a young boy with suspected appendicitis

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Homes
programme.desc: Jen and Alex's 1940s LA home is full of character and still has some of the original features, but its closed-off layout is no longer functioning for the growing family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: The team helps project manager Ben in Avening in the Cotswolds, whose life was changed in a split second by a freak accident while holidaying in Cyprus. Ben slipped and fell head first into the shallow end of a pool, breaking his neck in three places, and for him to move back home from Salisbury Spinal Unit, it needs to be adapted with a front access and a two-storey extension with through-floor lift, along with all the bespoke care provision that he will need, including a home-office space

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In March 2015, football fan Simon Dobbin was at an away game when he was set upon by a gang of men in an unprovoked attack that left him brain damaged, paralysed and unable to walk and talk. After many months in hospital he is finally back at home in Mildenhall, Suffolk, where he receives round-the-clock care from wife Nicole. But the family home is unfit for Simon's care and rehabilitation - so that's where Nick Knowles, designer Laurence Llewelyn- Bowen and the DIY SOS team come in, strengthened by a number of local tradesmen

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Hessle, near Hull in East Yorkshire, to transform the home of Darren Hudson, who was left paralysed from the waist down due to a spinal cord injury. After three months of hospital rehabilitation, Darren returned home to his family, but tragedy struck again eight days later when his wife Sarah died suddenly. Since that moment, Darren and his three sons have been living in emotional trauma, with the wheelchair-using dad now the main carer for the family

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel suspects that an 18-month-old Australian Shepherd dog may be suffering from nerve compression, and an 11-month-old pug shows signs of neurological damage. It transpires that she has bone malformation that is squashing her spinal cord. Elsewhere, there is an emergency admission when a Jack Russell puppy called Bella is rushed in with a crushed pelvis after a freak accident

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A French bulldog called Dave has a spinal deformity, and Noel discovers that not only does he have a genetic malformation of his sacral and lumbar vertebrae, but also excess bone in the pathways where the nerves travel to his legs. Meanwhile, a six-year-old terrier has lost a paw after being attacked by another dog, and the only options appear to be replacing the paw with a bionic implant or amputating the entire limb

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets a puppy who needs urgent treatment for a badly broken leg and a young Doberman who has spent most of her life in quarantine kennels

programme.title: DIY SOS

Ben slipped and fell head first into the shallow end of a pool, breaking his neck in three places, and for him to move back home from Salisbury Spinal Unit, it needs to be adapted with a front access and a two-storey extension with through-floor lift, along with all the bespoke care provision that he will need, including a home-office space

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Journalist and TV personality Piers Morgan continues his travels across the USA, this time meeting two very different female murderers, both of whom killed someone they loved. First, he heads to Florida's largest female prison to visit Amanda Hutto, who is serving a life sentence for murdering her daughter. She was accused of deliberately 'dunking' her seven-year-old child as a punishment for being naughty, with her young son testifying against her at the murder trial. Next, he journeys to Texas, where he encounters Rhonda Glover, a former beauty queen and rodeo star who gunned down her lover in cold blood after their relationship spiralled into drug-fuelled obsession and paranoia

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A luxury hotel chain makes penniless and exhausted couple Mike and Alison an offer they can't refuse, but the prospect of their departure drives a wedge between the ghosts, and the hardline Captain finds himself ostracised, while Pat is voted in as reluctant leader. As the clueless spirits brainstorm money-making schemes, the Captain takes matters into his own hands. Meanwhile, fortune smiles on Headless Humphrey in his ceaseless search for his errant body

programme.title: Deadly Waters with Captain Lee
programme.desc: In the wake of a violent storm, a Rhode Island harbourmaster finds a badly decomposed body aboard a sailboat

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.sub-title: The Death Cleaners vs the Sister Council
programme.desc: A couple who have raised eight children are ready to embrace the death cleaning process, but the children are against the move

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: V and Ollie blue light to a rugby pitch to help a man who had a seizure. Plus, Stef and Lee treat an excitable 6-year-old Spider-Man fighting an asthma attack

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise for Derek in Hull - an MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus has his eye on a Citroen XM that looks amazing - but it's in France. If they can double their money, it'll be worth the trip, but the car has some big problems. Will isn't sure, but the engineering challenges win him over

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Aspiring makers are given the opportunity to turn their hobbies and skills into money-making businesses, joining Dom Chinea at a training camp in the grounds of an Arts and Crafts house in Leicestershire. Izzy has given up a career in advertising to try to become a full-time potter. Stone carver Luke needs help overcoming a serious confidence problem that is holding him back

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Aspiring makers are given the opportunity to turn their hobbies and skills into money-making businesses. With chronic fatigue syndrome, autism and attention deficit hyperactivity disorder Dea is hoping her willow-weaving can help her build a home business. For artistically inclined Gemma, it's all about proving her careers advisor wrong. Dom Chinea presents

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husband's treasured collection of Rolls-Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a car auction business. A near-perfect 50s Chevy panel van and a very desirable low-mileage 80s Brooklands Capri tempt auctioneer Derek out of Yorkshire

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at how corruption became rife within the SS, with the money stolen from Jews finding its way into the pockets of the camp guards rather than funding the war effort. Many of the SS also enjoyed illicit sexual relationships with female prisoners. Meanwhile, Dr Mengele embarked on a gruesome series of experiments, using children as guinea pigs in his research into genetic inheritance

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Party's militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Lancashire, where they examine items in the Blackpool Tower Ballroom. The antiques include a Bible containing handwritten extracts by Charlotte Bronte, images drawn under fire by a war artist and a valuable brooch

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to the Tower Ballroom in Blackpool, where items valued include one of the oldest prayer books examined on the programme, a valuable pair of duelling pistols and the original teddy from the BBC children's programme Watch with Mother

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise for Derek in Hull - an MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming's exploration of the English canal network continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester. But disaster strikes when he runs aground, threatening to stop him from reaching his final destination

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A CBD80 battery-powered loco needs to be hauled 20 miles from a factory in Burton-on-Trent to the heritage Chasewater Railway in Staffordshire

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Adam takes a gamble and leaves the English Channel in the hope of finding Dover sole, one of the most expensive fish in British waters, to help cover his rising financial issues. Up in Scotland, Stephen breaks from the rest of the fleet for some solo fishing but heads towards some stormy weather, leaving him with a decision to make

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a Ford Thames 400E that once belonged to the pop band Edison Lighthouse. Dave heads to York to collect an Astra GTE convertible

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will find an Audi Quattro roadster than has seen better days ? it?s full of problems, and hasn?t been serviced in years. Even so, they are intent on renovating it

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: A textile designer from Fife who insists on using sustainable fabrics and a Devon glassblower picking up her craft after an eight-year break try to turn a profit from their skills

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea gives blacksmith Alex and furniture-maker Cora the opportunity to turn their hobbies and skills into money-making businesses. Although incredibly passionate about his craft, Alex suffers with imposter syndrome but mentor Adrian Wood is hoping to help him see the beauty in his work. Cora gave up her day job, determined to follow her dreams but dislikes the small details and often jumps to the next job before finishing the first. Mentor Alice thinks Cora has great taste and believes her best chance at making her dreams reality is to focus on the designing rather than making every piece herself

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: The team struggles to explain how a DAF 33 gearbox works, and office manager Sarah attempts to bring order to the auto memorabilia sale

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Anglophile Derek struggles to get his message across on a rare trip abroad to pick up a Bentley, while a well-restored Ford XR2 sets the auction alight

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: The events of 1944, when the death toll rose substantially. By this point, the Allies were aware of Auschwitz's purpose, and faced a difficult decision over what should be done about it. Plus, the story of the occupation of Hungary, revealing how Adolf Eichmann offered to spare a million Jews in return for certain services

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Documentary examining the rule of Adolf Hitler and his party, focusing on the Eastern Front during the Second World War, which many see as the defining arena of the conflict. In the autumn of 1940, Adolf Hitler has to decide what to do about the Soviet Union and its leader Joseph Stalin. He offers to carve up the post-war world in exchange for his fellow dictator's support in the war against Britain, but is actually preparing an attack on the USSR

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team present the programme from Hertford College, Oxford, where items valued include a bracelet once worn by a princess, expensive ceramics and a rare document recording the end of hostilities in the First World War

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to the Tamar Valley in Devon, and weigh anchor at Morwellham Quay

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up a rare Ford Zodiac Farnham Estate, while Dave heads for an isolated tent in the Midlands to collect a Triumph Toledo

programme.desc: Derek collects a Ford Thames 400E that once belonged to the pop band Edison Lighthouse. Dave heads to York to collect an Astra GTE convertible

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but he experiences engine issues and a leaky boat along the way

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed as he travels along the Shropshire Union Canal and discovers industrial secrets in Audlem, Cheshire

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: This week tells the tale of the two Mercs, but what will the moral of the story be? Plus, the team get stuck into a moddable favourite - a 2006 Ford Fiesta ST

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: In the 100th episode, Derek heads to Northumberland to collect a rare 1975 NSU RO 80, Jack deals with a Mini Mayfair stuck in a barn, and an Aston Martin DBS husk is auctioned

programme.title: Resto My Ride
programme.desc: Pete loved to take his XW Falcon Wagon out on the country roads with his grandmother. When she passed, she left him money and instructions to get it restored

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads out to pick up a pair of beautiful Swedish models - a Saab 96L and a Volvo P1800ES. Plus, a rare British AC Aceca comes up for sale

programme.title: Classic Car Kings
programme.desc: Big Irv has had a life-long love affair with VWs, so he's extremely surprised when he's offered the chance to work on a very early 1949 Beetle

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit landed gent Sir Benjamin Slade as they search for more upcycling prospects. Simon converts a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround, while Henry refurbishes a club fender fire surround and turns a quick profit on a Fordson tractor

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to the Tower Ballroom in Blackpool, where items valued include one of the oldest prayer books examined on the programme, a valuable pair of duelling pistols and the original teddy from the BBC children's programme Watch with Mother

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up

programme.title: Bangers & Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise for Derek in Hull - an MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at how corruption became rife within the SS, with the money stolen from Jews finding its way into the pockets of the camp guards rather than funding the war effort. Many of the SS also enjoyed illicit sexual relationships with female prisoners. Meanwhile, Dr Mengele embarked on a gruesome series of experiments, using children as guinea pigs in his research into genetic inheritance

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Party's militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester. But disaster strikes when he runs aground, threatening to stop him from reaching his final destination

Although incredibly passionate about his craft, Alex suffers with imposter syndrome but mentor Adrian Wood is hoping to help him see the beauty in his work. Cora gave up her day job, determined to follow her dreams but dislikes the small details and often jumps to the next job before finishing the first. Plus, the story of the occupation of Hungary, revealing how Adolf Eichmann offered to spare a million Jews in return for certain services

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Documentary examining the rule of Adolf Hitler and his party, focusing on the Eastern Front during the Second World War, which many see as the defining arena of the conflict. In the autumn of 1940, Adolf Hitler has to decide what to do about the Soviet Union and its leader Joseph Stalin. He offers to carve up the post-war world in exchange for his fellow dictator's support in the war against Britain, but is actually preparing an attack on the USSR

programme.sub-title: The Egg She Wishes to Crack
programme.desc: Another body gives Jeanette new leads, while Sophia is haunted by a patient. Crime drama, starring Katherine Kelly, Eve Myles and Dougray Scott

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When a car with a young girl in the back seat is stolen, the team investigates whether it was a crime of opportunity or a targeted abduction

programme.title: Protection
programme.desc: Witness protection officer Liz pursues her unofficial investigation into DI Brandice and his possible connection with organised crime, but it becomes increasingly difficult when the unit comes under pressure. Drama, starring Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Jenny 'The Vixen' Ryan, Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty, Shaun 'The Dark Destroyer' Wallace, Mark 'The Beast' Labbett and Darragh 'The Menace' Ennis come together to take on contestants from across the country. Can any of them come out on top? Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: A Mother's Son
programme.desc: First of a two-part drama, starring Hermione Norris, Martin Clunes, Paul McGann and Alexander Arnold. The murder of a schoolgirl rocks the residents of a quiet coastal town in Suffolk, and when Rosie stumbles upon a pair of bloodstained trainers hidden in her home, she suspects someone in her family may have been involved in the killing. With her son Jamie and stepson Rob both acting suspiciously, could she be right?

programme.title: Litvinenko
programme.desc: Watched by the world, the Metropolitan Police follow the radioactive trail of Polonium across London, leading them to their main suspects, Lugovoy and Kovtun. Drama, based on real-life events, starring David Tennant

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: Amy scrambles for answers with one person dead and another injured on board her flight home. Kirsten puts herself in greater danger when she agrees to meet with Sutherland and when she discovers the full scale of the conspiracy, Amy must decide if she's prepared to risk everything to find those responsible

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol is smitten with handsome visitor Major Giles MacLean, but Dawson, Wetherby and Mason suspect he could be involved in a spate of burglaries targeting the wealthiest homes in the district. Meanwhile, Peggy sets David up with an old friend of hers, a former circus performer, and Bernie worries when Rosie fails to return from Australia. Lisa Kay, Clare Wille, Rupert Ward-Lewis and Joe McFadden star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke travel to Tooting in south London to turn live-wire owner Zoe's tired and boring garden into the perfect outdoor entertaining space

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare classic festive dishes, including a fondue served with filo-wrapped asparagus and pancetta-covered potatoes. Lisa then shares her classic nut roast recipe, dressed up with a festive wreath, and John cooks cured salmon with aromatic spices, while for dessert the pair make hazelnut roulade with amaretto cream and a mulled wine compote

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: The Australian singer springs pleasant surprises on unsuspecting homeowners as he presents property makeovers. In the first edition, Peter oversees the transformation of a grandmother's cottage in Halstead, Essex, to which designer John Amabile brings Rococo glamour and country-estate chic

programme.title: Alex Polizzi's Secret Italy
programme.desc: Alex embarks on a voyage of discovery back to her roots as she revisits some of the places that have special memories for her as well as exploring some of the country's little-known treasures. She begins with a tour of northern Italy to uncover some of its hidden secrets including a prison in Venice with a difference and ventures to a gem in Lombard Alps, before taking in the fashionable sights that Italy's capital of cool Milan has to offer - here she gets the chance to admire some of opera singer Maria Callas's unforgettable stage costumes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick has to go undercover following the unlikely event of a gangster setting up shop in rural Aidensfield - and luckily his former girlfriend Jill is able to help out

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The world's tallest roller coaster in Los Angeles' Six Flags Amusement Park, which was inspired by scenic railways and steam catapults on aircraft carriers

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson helps sisters Sharon and Joy with their struggling country house hotel in Worlington, Suffolk. While the Grade-II listed building is in good repair, the operating systems are chaotic and the menu too extensive for the solitary chef to handle. Ruth organises a revamp of the dated-looking function room - prompting the delighted owners to plan a wedding fair in their new-look space

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn is in a downward spiral, while Sam wants a secret word with Ross, and Aaron makes it clear that he suspects Joe is the arsonist

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Lisa and George query Summer's story, Ben loses his temper with Maggie during a family lunch and Daniel becomes a target for online abuse. Roy reopens the refurbished cafe

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The casualty ward is flooded when a cowboy builder disconnects the wrong pipes - leading to injuries, chaos and a huge repair bill. Jack's fishing trip comes to an abrupt end when a car crashes into the lake, and Jean finds her battle to raise funds for the hospital is about to get much more difficult. Starring Gareth Hale, Glynis Barber and Wendy Craig

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A mysterious fire that killed an artist with links to political activists leaves the police baffled, while Bright finds himself short-staffed when a stomach bug puts most of his men on sick leave. The situation becomes more serious when a housewife dies of the same condition. Endeavour investigates a family-run supermarket, believing the infection may have stemmed from their stock being deliberately contaminated, and discovers dark secrets that may have a connection to the unexplained blaze

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A woman seeks justice when her husband suffers a fatal heart attack after confronting a burglar. Meanwhile, Greengrass prepares for the gooseberry competition at the village fair

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: After a strict headteacher is murdered during a PTA meeting, school scandals and nervous witnesses complicate the hunt for a killer. Mystery drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, with Nick Hendrix, Annette Badland and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary lends Summer an ear, Jodie plants a suspicion in Daniel's head and Sarah's behaviour raises eyebrows. Christina seeks George's help after feeling the long arm of the law

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: The Suspect
programme.desc: Joe is knocked sideways when an old grudge rears its head and with the police closing in, he flees to his dad's house for sanctuary. Ruiz has a nagging doubt that he is missing something - he just can't put his finger on it, while Joe must dig deep into his own past to understand what is happening in the present

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: The detective and his team are called to the site of a fatal crash involving an articulated lorry, a car and a blue pick-up truck. An unexpected discovery found on the victim at the scene soon suggests there could be far more to this particular accident than meets the eye, sparking an unsettling chain of events that leads Grace and Branson on a game of cat and mouse through Brighton to save an innocent life before time runs out

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson try to trace Rosie's whereabouts in Australia, and Mick becomes a suspect when they uncover evidence of domestic violence in his relationship with her. Meanwhile in Brisbane, Carol visits a monastery to find out more about her long-lost brother, but her search leads to a trap when she receives a call from someone claiming to have information. Starring Joe McFadden, Clare Wille and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective sets out for Norfolk when a country publican begs him to help solve a murder - but is disappointed when the innkeeper turns out to be a budding author and the killing fictional. However, he soon has food for thought when a real death occurs - committed by a seemingly ghostly hand. Geraldine Alexander and Ian McCulloch guest star, with David Suchet

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke tackle a neglected and boring backyard in Birmingham for its owner Sophie, who wants the area full of birds, bees and butterflies

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare food for the big day, beginning with eggs royale with homemade Hollandaise sauce. John gives a turkey masterclass, while Lisa cooks up some sumptuous sides with her red cabbage, leafy brussels and fail-safe roast potatoes. For dessert, there are little ginger puddings and a cardamom custard

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Interior designer Linda Barker joins presenter Peter Andre and the team to transform the home of teenager Jack Payne in Colchester, Essex, and surprise him by hanging furniture from the ceiling

programme.title: Alex Polizzi's Secret Italy
programme.desc: The presenter explores the secrets of the relatively undiscovered Le Marche region, before heading to the capital Rome and a rendezvous with former boyfriend Luca. She also visits her late grandmother's home in the hillside village of Monforte

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A woman seeks justice when her husband suffers a fatal heart attack after confronting a burglar. Meanwhile, Greengrass prepares for the gooseberry competition at the village fair

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How an oil rig in the Gulf of Mexico was inspired by the first drilled oil well, pioneering remote manipulator arms, and the discovery of fireproof chemicals

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn is found unconscious at the foot of the stairs, while a desperate Charity offers her half of the pub to Kim, and the search for the arsonist continues

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Jean announces that Casualty may have to close in light of a financial package to repair the department being rejected. With no admissions or emergency surgery, a round of cost-cutting redundancies seems inevitable - until a mystery benefactor offers to help. When an unexploded wartime bomb detonates, Jill and Ralph have to operate on a tramp trapped in the wreckage of a collapsed buildinge. Guest starring George Irving

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the disappearance of a Dutch au pair, which seems connected to rivalry amid the city's academics. His search leads him to a family estate in the Oxfordshire countryside where he meets three siblings divided by personal demons and shared loss. Endeavour suspects there is a link between the disappearance and an unsolved case from years before, but as the investigation continues, conflict erupts between his fellow officers. When a group of teenagers makes a horrifying discovery, he suspects someone is manipulating events from afar

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When Greengrass finds an unexploded bomb the Army is called in to deal with it, but one woman refuses to leave her home. Police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: The Au Pair
programme.desc: Following a disturbing incident, Zoe finds herself increasingly on the outside of her own family. As doubts about her judgement grow, she throws herself into preparing a meaningful gift for her stepdaughter Amber's birthday. Meanwhile, Amber begins to question Sandrine's true intentions, setting her on a dangerous path towards the truth

programme.desc: Marco fights for a position in the Walsh clan empire, but when Toby uncovers the truth he risks causing the walls to come crashing down

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: A brutal robbery at a secluded Brighton house pulls Grace and Branson into a challenging enquiry when thousands of pounds worth of antiques are stolen and the owner is left fighting for her life. The prime target of the incident appears to have been an item of great sentimental value to the homeowner and her brother and has deep emotional ties to a mystery from the 1960s involving their father. Return of the crime drama, starring John Simm

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: Rising music star Gaia Lafayette announces to her devoted fans that she and her band are headed to Los Angeles, but a shocking attack leads Grace and Branson to believe someone may be trying to harm her. With a perpetrator on the loose, Roy must deduce who of Gaia's fanbase may be concealing sinister intentions. Meanwhile, a grisly discovery on a remote Sussex pig farm puts the detective under serious pressure, forcing him to split his team in order to investigate two impossible lines of enquiry. Crime drama, starring John Simm

programme.title: Genius Game
programme.desc: The eliminated players return to pledge their allegiance, giving their chosen player an advantage in the final game. The finalists face a best-of-three Death Match format that tests the most strategic and lateral thinkers and the first player to win two games will be the winner

programme.title: Genius Game
programme.desc: With only five players remaining, there is everything to play for in the Main Match Media Moguls, a game where only the most astute investors will be rewarded. The winner of the Main Match will automatically secure a place in the final while the loser faces an immediate elimination - as all players vie for the top spot, not everyone is honest about their strategy. David Tennant hosts

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Katherine from East Lothian, Rachel from London, Tom from Oxford and Nick from West Yorkshire pit their wits against one of the ruthless experts in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Salvage Squad
programme.desc: Brian Dowling and his fashion-forward design team continue their mission to prove to the nation that it is possible achieve a dream home renovation at little cost. Changing Rooms meets the Repair Shop with a new sustainable twist, the challenge here is to transform our bland old rooms, furniture and fittings into unique and stunning show stoppers, using only upcycling and sustainable methods. This is the home makeover format for the world we live in today, as two households per week, each with a team consisting of an interior designer and an up-cycling wiz, have just four days and 1,000 euro to renovate and rejuvenate a tired spaces in their home, filling them with home-made, re-used and revamped furnishings. Two families are selected from the same region each week and each has a tired room in need of renovation and re-imagining containing a potential treasure trove of up-cycling gems

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Quiz game show hosted by Lee Mack in which the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts, but are all about logic and common sense. A hundred contestants begin every show, but who will make it to the end and answer a question only one per cent of the country can get right - and win up to £100,000?

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The final four chefs are given just ten minutes and 25 dollars to shop for a challenge with a brutal twist: they're selecting for each other.

programme.title: Undercover Girlfriends
programme.desc: Five men who think they are taking part in a reality TV show in Marbella are actually being watched by their partners, who are in control of the whole thing. Everywhere the boys go and everything they do is controlled by their girlfriends, who use stealth, technology and a range of comical disguises to put their boyfriends to the test to answer some important questions about their relationships

programme.title: Olivia Attwood: The Price of Perfection
programme.desc: The presenter and model meets brave women willing to discuss their insecurities about their vaginas and witnesses procedures to help them

programme.title: Bump
programme.desc: Reema and Oly try to figure out how their friendship works, now that they have one big thing not in common

programme.title: My Mum, Your Dad
programme.desc: A new dad causes a stir as he gets to know the mums with some speed dating. Davina heads to the Retreat to catch up with the parents after their first dates and some of them share their vulnerabilities around finding love. One mum opens up as she reveals the reason her marriage broke down and the kids choose who they want their parents to spend some extra one-on-one time with while they cook the evening meal

programme.title: The Chase Australia
programme.desc: Andrew O'Keefe presents the Down Under version of the quiz show in which four people must work together to beat the Chaser and win a cash prize
